description_long: This is an electric guitar with a distinctive offset body shape, reminiscent of the Gibson Firebird design, featuring a sunburst finish that transitions from a deep brown at the edges to a lighter amber in the center. The front face displays a white pickguard with visible screws and a signature or logo, as well as two control knobs and a bridge with metallic hardware. The neck is long and slender, with rectangular mother-of-pearl inlays on a dark fretboard, and the headstock is angled with six inline tuning pegs. The back of the guitar continues the sunburst finish, showing a smooth, contoured surface without any electronics cavity covers. The side views reveal the guitar's slim profile and the raised pickguard and hardware on the front. This instrument is typically constructed from solid wood with metal and plastic components, designed for use in musical performances or as an urban street instrument.
description: A sunburst-finished electric guitar with an offset body, white pickguard, and six-inline headstock, featuring metallic hardware and rectangular fretboard inlays.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sunburst body, white pickguard, control knobs, bridge, and detailed fretboard inlays.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the guitar's slim body and neck, with tuning pegs visible along the headstock edge.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, contoured sunburst back and the full length of the neck and headstock.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the thinness of the body and neck, with hardware slightly protruding from the front.
attribute_headstock_design: Angled headstock with six inline tuning pegs, no visible logo, elongated and slightly asymmetrical shape
